scope: Hygiene milestone — restore `cargo test --workspace --features semantic` to all-GREEN baseline (modulo 33 out-of-scope vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forwards). Three reframes from the original M6 charter: (1) ZERO build failures at HEAD 671a984 — the M6 '26 test-target build failures' claim is a category mislabel for '26 test binaries with FAILED test result' (live capture confirms cargo build --workspace --tests --features semantic exit 0). (2) The W2-pre-report 16-name pre_existing_orthogonal_failures list is a SUBSET — live capture surfaces 276 runtime failures across 26 binaries. (3) ~200 of 276 failures are tests for ARCHIVED subcommands (cfg, dfg, ssa, alias, dominators, live_vars, abstract_interp, arch, behavioral, bounds, diff_impact, equivalence, maintainability, mutability, purity, search, secrets, gvn-equivalent — all moved to crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/archived/ in prior milestones; their CLI-test-file invocations were left dangling). The plan: M1 authoritative RED-capture + enumeration; M2 fix 4 doctests; M3 ATOMIC delete ~200 archived-cmd test functions (mirror vuln-migration-v1 M5 pattern); M4 fix ~16-25 orthogonal-real Cat-C failures (fixture gaps, parser regressions, error-path hardening, cognitive-analyzer SonarQube alignment, git-log empty-repo handling, change-impact only-remote-exists hint); M5 CHANGELOG + local tag. Net LOC delta approximately -3300. ZERO #[ignore] markers (no genuinely-environmental failures). Penultimate milestone before external publish — both this AND vuln-source-parity-v1 sibling must land before single coherent cargo publish.

validator_mandates.no_test_gaming_rule: Per /Users/cosimo/.claude/CLAUDE.md global instructions: every test failure disposition is FIX or DELETE-on-genuine-obsolescence. ZERO #[ignore] markers permitted in this milestone. ZERO weakened assertions. ZERO commented-out code. DELETE only allowed when (a) target functionality is intentionally archived/removed AND (b) modern equivalent exists in active codebase. Both conditions must be documented per deleted test in M3 commit message body. The full enumeration of dispositions is plan §5 (decision matrix); operator may audit any deletion against the matrix.
validator_mandates.m1_enumeration_authoritative: M1 produces TWO authoritative JSON artifacts: (a) reports/M1-archived-cmd-test-enumeration.json — every Cat-A test function by file + line + invoked-archived-subcmd, schema {file, line, fn_name, archived_subcommand}, estimated ~200 entries; (b) reports/M1-orthogonal-real-failures.json — every Cat-C test by file + line + root_cause + disposition + fix_sketch, estimated 16-25 entries. M3 sources its deletion list FROM (a); M4 sources its fix list FROM (b). Numerical reconciliation gate at M1: |Cat-A| + |Cat-B (33 vuln_migration_v1_red)| + |Cat-C| + 4 (doctests) MUST equal the live-capture total at HEAD (276 per investigation.json or whatever M1 RED capture observes; allow +/- 5 drift across runs).
validator_mandates.m3_atomic_deletion_required: M3 (bulk delete of archived-cmd tests) MUST ship in ONE commit. atomic_commit:true; release_commit_group:milestone_3_atomic. Mirrors vuln-migration-v1 M5 ATOMIC pattern. Without atomicity, intermediate states leave compile failures or orphan helpers. Rollback rule: if post-commit cargo build/clippy fails, REVERT entire M3 commit + re-investigate.
validator_mandates.m3_active_subcmd_preservation_mandatory: M3's source of truth for 'ACTIVE subcommand' is the Subcommand enum in crates/tldr-cli/src/main.rs. The full set of active variants is enumerated in plan §M3.green_files.preserved. Any test invoking `tldr <X>` where X is in that enum is PRESERVED unconditionally. Any test invoking `tldr <X>` where X is NOT in that enum is DELETED. The classification is mechanical — no judgment calls. M1 enumeration is the gate.
validator_mandates.category_b_out_of_scope: The 33 `<lang>_<vuln_type>_positive` failures in vuln_migration_v1_red.rs are EXCLUDED from this milestone. They are M3-CF-01 + M4-CF-01 source-bank-gap RED tests, owned by the sibling vuln-source-parity-v1 milestone. M1's reports/M1-out-of-scope-confirmation.json EXPLICITLY lists all 33 by name + file + line and cross-references the sibling milestone. M3 + M4 do NOT touch these tests. M4 stop-threshold expects exactly 33 remaining failures (Category-B only) after Cat-A + Cat-C are eliminated.
validator_mandates.cli_output_shape_unchanged: tldr CLI public-API surface MUST NOT change. Subcommand enum in main.rs preserved as-is (this milestone deletes TESTS for subcommands, not subcommand definitions or implementations). JSON / SARIF / text output schemas unchanged. Exit codes unchanged. Help text unchanged. M4 source-code edits are surgical and targeted (cognitive analyzer else-handling, parse_coverage empty-input, analyze_dead_code empty-input, compute_martin_metrics empty-input, git_log no-commits-yet handling, change-impact NoBaseline hint substring) — none of which are part of the documented public API contract.
validator_mandates.no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_imple: M2-M4 source changes are restricted to: (a) doctest edits (4 files); (b) test fixtures (.gitkeep, test-helper modifications); (c) the explicit M4 list of internal helpers (cognitive.rs, dead_code.rs, martin.rs, coverage.rs, git_log helper, NoBaseline error construction). NO active-subcommand command-handler modifications. NO parser modifications. NO output-format modifications. NO tree-sitter grammar updates.
validator_mandates.pre_publish_baseline_restored: M4 final stop-threshold: `cargo test --workspace --features semantic --no-fail-fast --release` failure count = 33 EXACTLY (Category-B vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forwards only). ZERO Category-A residue (all archived-cmd tests deleted). ZERO Category-C residue (all orthogonal-real failures fixed or deleted-on-stale). ZERO doctest failures (M2 fix preserved). If failure count > 33, M4 BLOCKED.
validator_mandates.no_test_deletion_silently: Every M3 deletion (Cat-A) documented in commit message body with file + function name + rationale (=archived-subcmd-name + reference to crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/archived/<X>.rs). Every M4 DELETE-on-stale (Kotlin/Swift unsupported tests) similarly documented. Operator can audit by reading the commit body — no opaque deletions.
validator_mandates.cargo_lock_never_staged: Cargo.lock NEVER staged in any commit of this milestone. `git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty.
validator_mandates.staging_method_explicit_add_only: Each commit stages ONLY files explicitly listed in the milestone's green_files + the milestone-plan-directory artifacts. Verification: `git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!workspace-test-infrastructure-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK`.
validator_mandates.no_push_no_publish: Local commits + local tag ONLY. ZERO `git push`. ZERO `cargo publish`. ZERO version bumps. The publish-operator owns the cargo publish step downstream; this milestone produces an internal-tag-only artifact.
validator_mandates.operator_escalation_on_regression_from_p: If M1 RED capture identifies a Cat-C failure whose root cause is a regression introduced by one of the prior 4 internal milestones (regex-removal-v1, field_access_info-extension-v1, sanitizer-removal-v1, vuln-migration-v1), the worker MUST STOP and write reports/operator-escalation-regression.json detailing the regression + suspected commit + recommended action (revert candidate commit, much bigger scope). DO NOT silently fix a regression that should trigger a milestone-level revert decision.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!workspace-test-infrastructure-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts; only this plan's files + the source/test edits per milestone are eligible. Cargo.lock NEVER staged.

NO partial-fix follow-up commits — preserves atomic boundary.

green_files.path: (crates/tldr-core/src/quality/churn.rs OR crates/tldr-core/src/git.rs OR equivalent — M1 RED capture identifies)
green_files.anchor: git_log function (called from test_git_log_empty_repo via git_log(temp_dir.path(), 30, "%H", &[]))
additions: HARDEN git_log to handle the no-commits-yet condition gracefully: when `git log` returns exit-128 with stderr containing 'does not have any commits yet' (or equivalent locale strings), return Ok(String::new()) instead of bubbling up as Err.
RATIONALE: The test's behavior contract (Ok(empty) on empty repo) reflects intended UX — users running tldr against a fresh-init repo should get an empty changelog, not a hard error.
Per validator_mandate no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_implementations: this change is to an INTERNAL helper (`git_log`), not an active-subcmd command-handler. It is in the named exception list.

green_files.path: (crates/tldr-core/src/metrics/cognitive.rs OR crates/tldr-core/src/quality/cognitive.rs OR equivalent — M1 RED capture identifies)
additions: AUDIT the else-clause path. Per SonarQube cognitive complexity spec: `if x: return 1; else: return -1` cognitive == 1 (only the if adds; else does NOT). Test asserts cognitive == 1 but actual likely == 2.
FIX so the analyzer matches spec.
Per validator_mandate no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_implementations: internal helper change in named exception list.
On empty input directory, return Ok(<empty DeadCodeReport>) instead of Err. Vacuous-truth handling: no functions ⇒ no dead functions.
Same shape as dead_code: empty input → Ok with empty MartinReport.

green_files.anchor: parse_coverage empty-input handling + cobertura/lcov format alignment
additions: (a) Empty input directory → Ok(<empty CoverageReport>) instead of Err.

(b) Audit cobertura parser regression: fixture is named 'coverage.xml' with valid cobertura XML content; CoverageFormat::Cobertura hint passed; parser must recognize. Likely fix: ensure cobertura parser doesn't filter on filename suffix when format hint is explicit.
(c) Same for lcov: fixture 'coverage.lcov' with valid LCOV content; CoverageFormat::Lcov hint.
MAKE the two test fixture functions structurally distinct enough to fall below 0.8 similarity threshold. e.g., one has multi-statement body, one has single-statement body. NOT lower the threshold.
Per no-gaming rule: do NOT weaken assertion.

Per validator_mandate no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_implementations: ChangeImpact IS an active subcommand; this change is to its INTERNAL error-reason string, NOT to its CLI argument shape, JSON output, or exit code. It is in the named exception list. Public API contract preserved.

milestones.title: CHANGELOG entry + local tag

green_files.purpose: Internal-milestone entry per regex-removal-v1 / sanitizer-removal-v1 / vuln-migration-v1 precedent
sections: ### Removed: ~200 obsolete CLI integration tests for subcommands archived in prior milestones (cfg, dfg, ssa, gvn, alias, dominators, live_vars, abstract_interp, arch, behavioral, bounds, diff_impact, equivalence, maintainability, mutability, purity, search, secrets — already moved to crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/archived/ in earlier internal milestones; their CLI test invocations had been left dangling). Modern equivalents (taint, slice, whatbreaks, references, dead, hubs, etc.) retain full active test coverage.

### Removed: 2 obsolete test_*_returns_unsupported tests for Kotlin and Swift in crates/tldr-core/tests/language_parity_test.rs. Both languages are now SUPPORTED via tree_sitter_kotlin_ng and tree_sitter_swift (per crates/tldr-core/src/ast/parser.rs:136-137); replacement parse-success tests already exist (parser.rs:420-432).

### Fixed: 4 doctest failures in tldr-core — callgraph::cross_file_types::FuncIndexProxy (rewritten to use FuncIndexProxyMut working impl), callgraph::languages::kotlin::KotlinHandler::parse_import_node and callgraph::languages::luau::LuauHandler::extract_aliased_require (bare ``` → ```text fence), surface::triggers::extract_name_triggers (stale tldr_core::contracts::triggers::... import path → tldr_core::surface::triggers::...).

### Fixed: ~16-25 orthogonal-real test failures across tldr-core — empty-directory tree handling fixture gap, stale Ruby-unsupported assertion, git-log on no-commits-yet repo, cognitive-complexity else-not-counted SonarQube-spec alignment, empty-input handling for analyze_dead_code / compute_martin_metrics / parse_coverage, cobertura/lcov coverage parser regression, similarity-threshold fixture distinctness, change-impact only-remote-exists hint substring in NoBaseline error reason, and change-impact CLI test fixture git-repo setup.
### Retained: ALL active-subcommand CLI integration tests; ALL test_e2e_* vuln tests; ALL daemon-related tests; ALL semantic / fastembed / embedding tests; ALL non-archived tldr-core library tests.

### Test infrastructure baseline restored: After this milestone + vuln-source-parity-v1 (sibling), cargo test --workspace --features semantic --no-fail-fast --release is fully GREEN (modulo any newly-introduced regressions). The pre-publish baseline is restored.

### Architectural note: This is a HYGIENE milestone — no new features, no new test coverage, no public API changes. The single coherent external cargo publish (closing #7, #23, #24, #27, #28 + tldr vuln FP class + sanitizer correctness) is gated on this milestone + vuln-source-parity-v1 both landing.

green_files.purpose: Final summary: actual LOC delta sourced from `git diff --stat HEAD~N HEAD` (no manual estimation), actual archived-cmd-test count deleted (sourced from M3 commit stats), actual orthogonal-real-failure count fixed (sourced from M4-fix-by-fix-capture.json).

stop_thresholds: CHANGELOG entry merged.
Local annotated tag workspace-test-infrastructure-v1 applied (no push).
NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.
reports/M5-release-prep.md committed with actual-vs-estimated LOC delta + count comparison.
Final cargo test verification: cargo test --workspace --features semantic --no-fail-fast --release failure-count = 33 (Category-B only); 0 doctest failures; 0 build failures; 0 clippy warnings.

category_b_vuln_migration_v1_red_carry_f.rationale: These are M3-CF-01 + M4-CF-01 source-bank-gap RED tests carried forward from vuln-migration-v1 M5. They represent gaps in vuln source/sink banks (specific lang × vuln-type combinations where canonical compute_taint_with_tree does not yet detect known-positive cases). Fixing them requires AST sink-bank extension across multiple LanguagePatterns — distinct from this milestone's pure test-infrastructure-cleanup scope. M1 reports/M1-out-of-scope-confirmation.json EXPLICITLY enumerates all 33 by name + file + line and cross-references vuln-source-parity-v1 ownership.

build_failures_originally_chartered.rationale: M6 pre-publish-binary-verification.json claim of '26 test-target build failures' is a CATEGORY MISLABEL for '26 test BINARIES with FAILED test result'. Live verification confirms cargo build --workspace --tests --features semantic exit 0 at HEAD 671a984. This milestone has NO build-failure-fix sub-milestone.

external_cargo_publish.rationale: Out of scope. This is a pre-publish cleanup milestone. The publish operator runs `cargo publish` downstream after BOTH this milestone AND vuln-source-parity-v1 land.

version_bumps.rationale: No version bump in this milestone. Public API surface unchanged. The cargo publish (separate operator action) handles versioning.

active_subcmd_command_handler_modificati.rationale: Per validator_mandate no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_implementations: M2-M4 source changes are restricted to (a) doctest edits, (b) test fixtures, (c) the specific named internal helpers in M4 (cognitive analyzer, parse_coverage, analyze_dead_code, compute_martin_metrics, git_log, change-impact NoBaseline reason). NO active-subcommand command-handler modifications.

premortem_risks.R1_cat_a_misclassification: HIGH if not careful, LOW with M1 enumeration cross-check against main.rs Subcommand enum. Mitigation: M1 enumeration is mechanical — every assert_cmd::cargo::cargo_bin!('tldr').args(["<X>", ...]) is checked against the Subcommand enum; X-in-enum → preserve, X-not-in-enum → delete.
premortem_risks.R2_orphan_helper_imports_post_m3: MEDIUM. cargo build --workspace --tests + cargo clippy -D warnings catches every orphan after M3 deletion. Rollback rule documented for partial-fail recovery.
premortem_risks.R3_m3_atomic_violation: HIGH if not careful. Mandate: atomic_commit:true; rollback if post-commit cargo build/clippy/test fails.
premortem_risks.R4_cat_c_fix_collateral_damage: MEDIUM. Per-fix verification (M4-fix-by-fix-capture.json) catches if a fix transitions another test GREEN→RED. Worker reverts the regressing fix, re-investigates.
premortem_risks.R5_regression_from_prior_milestone: MEDIUM. If M1 RED capture identifies a Cat-C failure caused by a prior-milestone regression (regex-removal-v1, FAI, sanitizer, vuln-migration), worker MUST escalate via reports/operator-escalation-regression.json — may require revert of a prior commit (much bigger scope; out of bounds for this milestone). Per validator_mandate operator_escalation_on_regression_from_prior_milestone.
premortem_risks.R6_inadvertent_active_test_deletion: MEDIUM if M1 enumeration is sloppy. Mitigation: M3 commit message body documents EVERY deletion with rationale. Operator audit-friendly. Per validator_mandate no_test_deletion_silently.
premortem_risks.R7_changelog_drift_from_actual: LOW. M5 sources counts from `git diff --stat HEAD~N HEAD` rather than manual estimation.
premortem_risks.R8_cargo_lock_or_sibling_files_staged: LOW. staging_method explicit add only; verification grep-pattern in dispatch contract.
premortem_risks.R9_doctest_text_fence_renders_unexpected: LOW. ```text renders as preformatted text (same visual as ```rust without the compile attempt). Visual inspection in M2 review.
premortem_risks.R10_m4_fix_for_change_impact_requires_mo: MEDIUM. If change-impact tests fail for reasons beyond missing-git-repo-fixture (e.g., a real change-impact regression), scope creep is possible. Mitigation: time-box M4 investigation per failing test; if a fix would require >50 LOC change to active-subcmd command-handler, escalate vs DELETE-and-defer.

recommended_pre_autonomous_steps: Verify HEAD is at 671a984 (or whatever the most recent vuln-migration-v1 / vuln-source-parity-v1 supporting-artifacts commit is).
Confirm with operator that the 33 vuln_migration_v1_red Category-B failures are owned by vuln-source-parity-v1 sibling milestone and EXCLUDED from this milestone's scope.
Confirm publish operator alignment: this milestone + vuln-source-parity-v1 both must land before external cargo publish (issues #7/#23/#24/#27/#28 closure).
